name: Yew
description: Mature, React-like virtual-DOM framework with the largest ecosystem and community

description.value: Rust/Wasm framework for creating reliable and efficient web applications with a React-like component model and html! macro

license.comment: Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0 per packages/yew/Cargo.toml

latest-release.comment: 0.23.0 released 2026-03-10; preceded by 0.22.0 (2025-12) after a long gap since 0.21.0 (2023-09)

maintenance-status.comment: Repo pushed 2026-07-17; 0.22 and 0.23 released within the last ~8 months after a slower 2023-2025 period

maturity.comment: 8+ years old with the largest Rust web ecosystem and a stable, well-documented API, but still 0.x with breaking changes between minor releases

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: Maintained by the community-run yewstack organization; no single corporate sponsor

reactivity-model.comment: Builds a virtual DOM and diffs it against the previous render, React-style

component-model.comment: Both function components (with hooks, the recommended style) and legacy struct components implementing the Component trait

view-authoring.comment: Views authored with the html! macro

fine-grained-updates.comment: Uses virtual-DOM diffing, not fine-grained signal updates

async-await-in-components.comment: Async data loading supported via Suspense plus use_future/use_future_with hooks; components themselves are not async fns

global-state-solution.comment: Built-in context (provide/consume); third-party crates like yewdux are commonly used for larger stores

primary-build-tool.comment: Trunk is the recommended build tool; wasm-pack also usable

official-cli.comment: No first-party CLI; relies on the third-party Trunk toolchain

dev-server-hmr.comment: Trunk provides rebuild + browser auto-refresh (live reload); no view-level hot reload

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No reliably reproducible gzipped hello-world measurement under a documented opt-level=z/LTO/wasm-opt profile available; Yew is generally on the heavier end among Rust web frameworks but a methodical number was not obtained

js-interop.comment: Interops through the standard wasm-bindgen / web-sys / js-sys stack; gloo wrappers commonly used on top

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Well-documented wasm-bindgen usage with the gloo helper crates smoothing common browser APIs; still some boilerplate

server-side-rendering.comment: ServerRenderer renders components to HTML; docs note SSR is still experimental

static-site-generation.comment: No first-party build-time route pre-rendering; SSR renders on request rather than generating static files

hydration.comment: Renderer::hydrate rehydrates the whole tree from server-rendered HTML

server-functions.comment: No integrated isomorphic server-function macro; server logic must be wired via manual endpoints

streaming-ssr.comment: ServerRenderer::render_stream returns a stream of HTML chunks

backend-integration.comment: SSR is server-framework-agnostic; examples exist for Axum and Actix Web but Yew does not bind to a specific backend

isomorphic-routing.comment: yew-router runs on both client and (for SSR) server

official-router.comment: yew-router is maintained in-tree by the core team

dynamic-route-params.comment: Path segments like /post/:id captured via the Routable derive

typed-routes.comment: Routes defined as an enum with #[derive(Routable)], type-checked at compile time

route-guards.comment: No built-in navigation guard/redirect hook API; redirects done manually in component logic

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No first-party route-level code splitting; the app ships as a single wasm bundle

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling system; use external/plain CSS, Tailwind, or inline styles. The community stylist crate adds CSS-in-Rust/scoped styles

scoped-styles.comment: No built-in component-scoped styling; scoping requires the third-party stylist crate

component-library-ecosystem.comment: Several community UI kits exist (e.g. yew-bootstrap, patternfly-yew, material-yew) but no single dominant mature library

forms-inputs.comment: Controlled inputs driven by state + oninput callbacks; no built-in two-way binding or form-helper library

svg-canvas-support.comment: SVG elements authored directly in the html! macro; canvas via web-sys/NodeRef

suspense-loading-ui.comment: Built-in Suspense component with fallback UI for async resources

error-boundaries.comment: No dedicated error-boundary component in the framework

documentation-quality.comment: Structured docs site with tutorial, concept guides, and API docs on docs.rs; some SSR/advanced areas thinner

api-ergonomics.comment: Function components with hooks feel idiomatic and React-like; legacy struct components are more verbose

compile-times.comment: Macro-heavy html! adds overhead but comparable to peer Rust web frameworks for small apps

examples-count.comment: The examples directory contains well over 20 sample apps

interactive-playground.comment: No official online playground/REPL; learning happens via local examples

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated component/state inspector beyond generic browser wasm debugging

testing-support.comment: Documents testing with wasm-bindgen-test in a headless browser

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the yew crate; snapshot 2026-07-19

contributors.comment: Approximate GitHub contributor count (including anonymous); snapshot 2026-07-19

community-size.comment: Largest Rust web framework community by stars/contributors with an active Discord

ecosystem-crates.comment: Mature surrounding crates: yew-router, yewdux, stylist, gloo, plus multiple UI kits

notable-production-users.comment: No widely and reliably documented production users beyond demos; not asserting specific companies

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: No official full-stack meta-framework; SSR/hydration are built into yew itself rather than a separate kit
